claim: Rapamycin accelerates aging in fruit flies.

result.claim: Rapamycin accelerates aging in fruit flies.
result.reasoning: The claim that rapamycin accelerates aging in fruit flies is refuted by direct evidence. Paper 058e1ec4f94a6782be874544432746f287787f92 (Credibility Rating: 2.5/5, animal_study) explicitly states that "a short rapamycin treatment during early life can prolong lifespan in Mus musculus and Drosophila melanogaster." It also notes that "the same treatment at later time points has no effect on lifespan," indicating that rapamycin does not accelerate aging in fruit flies at any stage, and can in fact extend it under specific conditions. No evidence was found to support the claim that rapamycin accelerates aging in fruit flies.

evidence.text: The lifespan of flies maintained on a diet containing 20% yeast is much shorter than that of flies maintained on a diet containing 10% yeast.
evidence.source: Supplementation of essential amino acids suppresses age-associated sleep loss and sleep fragmentation but not loss of rhythm strength under yeast-restricted malnutrition in Drosophila.
